commit.message:
feat(omegaconf): Add deferred resolver support with double-pass resolution and custom user resolvers (#1371)

## Description

This PR re-adds and enhances the deferred resolver functionality for the
OmegaConf inventory backend, along with comprehensive tests and new
features.

### Related Issue
Fixes #

---

## Proposed Changes

### 🚀 Core Features

#### Deferred (Escaped) Resolver Support
- Implemented double-pass resolution in OmegaConf backend
- First pass resolves all interpolations; escaped ones (`\${...}`)
become unescaped
- Second pass resolves the previously escaped interpolations
- Added `process_literals()` function with `KAPITAN_LITERAL` markers to
protect interpolation syntax

#### User-Defined Custom Resolvers
- Support for custom resolvers via `inventory/resolvers.py`
- Resolvers can access `_root_` context for target-level data
- Must implement `pass_resolvers()` function returning a dict of `{name:
func}`

### 🔧 Improvements

#### Enhanced Error Handling
- Added detailed error logging with traceback in `OmegaConfInventory`
- Fixed handling of empty YAML files (`yaml.safe_load` returns `None`)
- Fixed handling of null values for `classes:`, `parameters:`,
`applications:`, and `exports:`

#### Kubernetes Resource Base Class
- New `Base` class in `lib/kubernetes/k8s.py` for Kadet components
- Helper classes: `ConfigMap`, `Secret`, `ComponentConfig`,
`ComponentSecret`
- Methods for setting API version, kind, name, labels, annotations,
namespace

---

## Files Changed

### Core Changes
- `kapitan/inventory/backends/omegaconf/__init__.py` - Double-pass
resolution, empty file handling
- `kapitan/inventory/backends/omegaconf/resolvers.py` - New resolvers,
literal marker processing

### Tests
- `tests/test_compile.py` - New `CompileTestResourcesOCOmegaconf` class
- `tests/test_inventory.py` - `InventoryTargetTestOmegaConfOC` tests
- `tests/test_omegaconf.py` - Comprehensive resolver and backend tests
(~500 new lines)

### New Test Resources (`tests/test_resources_oc/`)
- `inventory/resolvers.py` - Example custom resolvers (`get_suffix`,
`get_substr`, `mul`, `upper`, `capitalize`, `escape`, `vaultkv`,
`helm_dep`, `helm_input`)
- `inventory/targets/` - Test targets: `test-deferred.yml`,
`test-merge.yml`, `test-resolvers.yml`
- `inventory/classes/components/` - Test classes for deferred
evaluation, merging, and resolver functionality
- `lib/kubernetes/` - Kadet base classes for Kubernetes resources

---

## Usage Example

### Deferred Resolution
```yaml
parameters:
  deployment_template:
    name: \${parentkey:}  # Deferred - resolves to key name when merged
    
  deployments:
    web-app: ${merge:${deployment_template}, {replicas: 2}}
    # Result: name will be "web-app"
```

### Custom Resolvers (`inventory/resolvers.py`)
```python
def get_suffix(name: str):
    return name.split("-")[-1]

def pass_resolvers():
    return {"get_suffix": get_suffix}
```

### Conditional Logic
```yaml
parameters:
  feature_enabled: true
  config: ${if:${feature_enabled}, {key: value}}
```

---

## Docs and Tests

- [x] Tests added
- [x] Updated documentation

---

## Checklist

- [x] Deferred resolver functionality works with double-pass resolution
- [x] Custom user resolvers can be loaded from `inventory/resolvers.py`
- [x] Empty YAML files and null values are handled gracefully
- [x] New conditional resolvers (`if`, `ifelse`, `and`, `or`, `not`,
`equal`)
- [x] Comprehensive test coverage for all new features

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Alessandro De Maria <alessandro.demaria@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Danny Heinrich <danny.heinrich@flower.ai>
Co-authored-by: Ricardo Amaro <ricardo.amaro@gmail.com>

commit.message:
Fix #749: Enable on-demand creation of reference dependencies (#1389)

- Modified reveal() function in kapitan/refs/functions.py to
automatically create missing references that have generation functions
when they are revealed by another reference
- This solves the dependency ordering problem where public keys (using
||reveal:path|publickey) failed when the private key (||rsa or
||ed25519) didn't exist yet
- The fix works recursively for multi-level dependency chains

Changes:
- kapitan/refs/functions.py: Enhanced reveal() with RefFromFuncError
handling for on-demand reference creation, comprehensive docstring with
examples
- tests/test_refs.py: Added 4 comprehensive tests covering RSA keypairs,
Ed25519 keypairs, multi-level dependencies, and complex nested objects
- docs/references.md: Updated publickey documentation to clarify
single-compile capability and added Ed25519 example

---------

Co-authored-by: Danny Heinrich <danny.heinrich@flower.ai>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Ricardo Amaro <ricardo.amaro@gmail.com>

commit.message:
fix: 3D viewport ResizeObserver — update WebGL canvas on window resize

- Add worldRef to expose the OBC world to the resize handler
- ResizeObserver watches containerRef; calls world.renderer.resize() when
  AppWindow is dragged or resized (primary fix)
- Fallback path: manually calls Three.js setSize + updateProjectionMatrix when
  renderer.resize() is not available
- typeof window guard prevents SSR errors
- worldRef cleared on cleanup so stale resize calls are silently skipped
- Add overflow-hidden to outer wrapper to prevent canvas bleed through
  AppWindow resize-handle hit areas
- Reduce minHeight 400 → 300 (doesn't interfere with flex h-full parents)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add CLI-based Atlassian Confluence REST API toolkit

Add dual-mode support via MODE env var (gui/cli/test):
- MODE=gui (default): existing HTTP server with browser UI
- MODE=cli: Confluence REST API v2 CLI toolkit
- MODE=test: internal unit tests (base64 encoder)

CLI features:
- acli-style command hierarchy: confluence <resource> <action> [flags]
- 10 resources: auth, page, space, search, comment, label, version,
  attachment, property, bulk
- Auth: env vars (CONFLUENCE_SITE/EMAIL/TOKEN) or stored (auth login)
- Output: compact JSON default, --pretty for indented, --verbose for debug
- Pagination: --limit N and --all flags with cursor-based auto-pagination
- Error handling: structured JSON on stderr, exit codes 0-4
- HTTPS natively via WasmEdge QuickJS built-in rustls

Test infrastructure (TDD):
- 18 TAP-format test suites in tests/
- Mock Confluence server (tests/mock-server.js)
- Shared test helpers (tests/helpers.sh)

Also:
- Fix HTTP method case (WasmEdge QuickJS lowercase methods)
- Add -- separator in Dockerfile ENTRYPOINT for CLI flag passthrough
- Add CLI mode docs to README.md and README-ko.md
- Add commented CLI service example in docker-compose.yml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add boss power/results UI and calculation

Expose boss data and benchmark-based win estimates: update README with GitHub link, instructions, and explanation of how Boss Result is calculated. Update index.html to add a GitHub repo CTA, new styles for boss rows and result badges, and a note linking to Issues. Add bossPower fields for Season 4 and Season 5 rows and a bossWinReference benchmark. Implement getBossResult() to compute Reduced Power, matchup score, and return Yes/Maybe/No with row classes (color-coding). Update table rendering to show Boss Power and Boss Result columns when present and to apply row classes. Small UI/CSS tweaks for layout and link styling.

Co-Authored-By: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Last-War-Resistance-Calculator
repository.description: A simple static calculator for Last War: Survival that shows: Season-based resistance tables - Boss Power tables for boss levels - Damage Reduction (%) - Reduced Power after damage reduction - Boss Result for rows that include boss data

commit.message:
fix: seamless frontend-backend routing audit fixes

- Remove duplicate font preload (layout.tsx) — next/font/local handles it
- Traefik rule: tighten PathPrefix(/api,/v1) → (/api/v1,/v1) so /api/auth/* reaches Next.js
- Fix BACKEND_URL → NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL in integrations callback + triggers webhook route handlers (was undefined in prod)
- Add /api/composio/triggers/schema/[slug] Next.js proxy route (trigger-config-step was calling a non-existent route)
- Add /api/boq/analyze Next.js proxy route + POST /bim/boq/analyze backend endpoint with LiteLLM vision analysis
- CI/CD: add missing SSH deploy steps (ACA was never being updated on push)
- CI/CD: push images to GHCR in addition to ACR (docker-compose.prod.yaml uses ghcr.io images)

commit.message:
Fix score modifier popup clipped by cell overflow and buried under sibling cells (#306)

* Initial plan

* fix: score popup now appended to grid element to render above all tiles

The score modifier popup (e.g. "+3" for stars) was clipped by the
cell's overflow:hidden and couldn't render above sibling cells.

- Move popup from cell child → gridElement child (like .pet-walker)
- Use --popup-row/--popup-col CSS vars for grid-relative positioning
- Update CSS: remove .cell scope, position using same formula as pet-walker
- Update animation keyframes for new positioning origin
- Fix white-space: wrap (invalid) → white-space: nowrap
- Update tests to query popup from gridElement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace raw json.dumps with canonicalize_json in torch_security.py (#1225)

* Initial plan

* Fix 4 Dependabot security alerts: torch CVE-2025-32434, Pillow, cryptography, black

Security fixes:
- torch: Updated to >=2.6.0 (CVE-2025-32434 RCE fix) for Linux and macOS ARM64
  - macOS Intel (x86_64) DEPRECATED: no official torch 2.6.0+ wheels available
- Pillow: Updated minimum to >=10.3.0 (CVE-2024-28219 buffer overflow fix)
- cryptography: Updated minimum to >=46.0.5 (GHSA subgroup attack fix)
- black: Updated minimum to >=26.3.1 (arbitrary file writes fix)

Updated SECURITY.md with vulnerability documentation.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: remove vulnerable torch for macOS Intel entirely, fix Pillow upper bounds

- Remove vulnerable torch 2.5.1 fallback for macOS Intel (no insecure compromises)
- Add platform markers to ml-core-darwin.in for ARM64-only torch
- Fix Pillow upper bound to <12 consistently (match original constraint)
- Update SECURITY.md to clarify macOS Intel ML support is REMOVED, not deprecated

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address final code review: improve documentation and consistency

- Clarify macOS Intel as NOT SUPPORTED (not deprecated with fallback suggestion)
- Fix ml-core-darwin.in comment to explain architectural relationship with ml-core.in
- Make SECURITY.md Known Vulnerabilities section formatting consistent
- Add comment explaining Pillow upper bound alignment with lint requirements

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restore CAS determinism: revert to pinned torch==2.2.2 with runtime CVE mitigation

BREAKING: Reverts floating torch>=2.6.0 to pinned torch==2.2.2 for ADR-032 compliance

Changes:
- Restore torch==2.2.2 / torchvision==0.17.2 pins in ml-core*.in files
- Add torch_security.py module with safe_load() wrapper (weights_only=True)
- Add platform security check in platform_matrix.py
- Add macOS Intel security warning to bootstrap script
- Fix Pillow constraint to >=10.3.0,<13 (allows 12.1.1 in lockfiles)
- Pin cryptography==46.0.5 and black==26.3.1 for determinism
- Update SECURITY.md documenting runtime mitigation strategy

Security Model:
- CVE-2025-32434 mitigated at runtime via weights_only=True
- Preserves CAS identity and lockfile determinism per ADR-032
- All torch.load() calls must use safe_load() or weights_only=True

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: fix comments and path references

- Clarify torch version suffix stripping comment
- Improve security comment explaining macOS Intel platform support limitation
- Fix path reference to torch_security.py in ml-core.in

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add security enforcement layer: global torch.load protection, CI gate, CAS identity

CRITICAL SECURITY CHANGES:
1. Global torch.load enforcement via install_global_enforcement()
2. CI validation script check_unsafe_torch_load.py to detect violations
3. Security profile added to CAS identity (security_profile_hash)
4. Hard fail on macOS Intel ML stack (TP_ALLOW_MACOS_INTEL_ML bypass)

This ensures security mitigations are ENFORCED, not just documented.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: improve string detection, add GHSA ID, fix pickle_module warning

- Fixed docstring detection to track matching quote types
- Improved string literal detection with character-by-character scanning
- Added GHSA-9v9h-cgj8-h64p identifier to cryptography comment
- Added DeprecationWarning for pickle_module parameter
- Moved os import to module level in platform_matrix.py

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply Black formatting to torch_security.py and platform_matrix.py

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add CAS hash verification on read and schema version enforcement

CRITICAL CAS HARDENING:
1. Added verify_object() method to check artifact integrity
2. Added verify=True parameter to materialize() for hash verification on read
3. Added CAS_IDENTITY_SCHEMA_VERSION constant (adr-032-v2) for evolvability
4. Schema version is now included in CAS identity computation

This prevents silent corruption from poisoning the CAS and ensures
old artifacts are invalidated when CAS identity schema changes.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Quarantine corrupt CAS artifacts instead of deleting (forensic analysis)

Code review feedback:
- Changed corrupt artifact handling from delete to quarantine
- Moved corrupt files to root/quarantine/{sha256}_{actual_sha16}
- Added CVE-2024-0727 reference to cryptography comment
- Applied Black formatting

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make security_profile canonical and verify=True default

CRITICAL FIXES for CAS determinism:
1. security_profile is now CANONICAL (static policy, no runtime state)
   - Removed enforcement_installed (runtime-derived) from profile hash
   - Added get_canonical_security_profile() with static policy values
   - Added assert_enforcement_installed() for validation

2. verify=True is now the default for materialize()
   - Prevents silent corruption propagation
   - verify=False emits warning

3. Added quarantine lifecycle policy
   - QUARANTINE_MAX_AGE_SECONDS = 7 days
   - QUARANTINE_MAX_SIZE_BYTES = 10 GB
   - Added gc_quarantine() for cleanup

These changes ensure:
- CAS identity is deterministic regardless of import order
- Corruption detection is ON by default
- Quarantine storage doesn't grow unbounded

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix type annotation: dict[str, any] -> dict[str, Any]

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix flake8 F824 errors in torch_security.py

Remove unnecessary 'global _original_torch_load' statements from
_enforced_torch_load() and _unsafe_torch_load_bypass() functions.
These functions only read the global variable but never assign to it,
so the global declaration is not needed and triggers F824 errors.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Resolve cas_store.py merge conflict with atomicity features

This merge resolution preserves:
- main's atomic-write (_atomic_write_file, _atomic_write_bytes)
- main's _fsync_parent_directory cross-platform helper
- main's CASFileLock for per-CAS-ID locking
- main's double-checked locking in add_file() / add_bytes()
- PR's quarantine/ layout and docs
- PR's verify_object() method
- PR's materialize(..., verify=True) default
- PR's corruption quarantine on read
- PR's gc_quarantine() method
- PR's quarantine lifecycle constants

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace raw json.dumps with canonicalize_json in torch_security.py

Fix CI lint failure: check_raw_json_usage.py detected raw json.dumps usage
at line 434. Replaced with canonicalize_json() from the canonical_json module,
which is the correct approach for deterministic JSON serialization for hashing.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comprehensive tests per code review feedback

- Add tests for CAS corruption-handling path (materialize with verify=True quarantines corrupt artifacts)
- Add tests for gc_quarantine() lifecycle policy (age-based and size-based deletion)
- Add tests for platform fingerprint security_profile field and include_security_profile=False
- Add tests for torch_security.py enforcement and hash determinism

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Black formatting for test_platform_security_profile.py

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix isort import ordering in test_platform_security_profile.py

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(core): implement OSC 104 reset color palette

Add OSC 104 support to VTParser. OSC 104 resets one or more color
palette entries (as set via OSC 4) back to their defaults.

- No-argument form (`OSC 104 ST`) resets the entire palette; the
  registered callback is invoked once with index -1.
- Parameterised form (`OSC 104;c1;c2 ST`) resets each listed index;
  the callback is invoked once per index with values 0-255.

Implementation:
- Added `onOsc104` private field and `setOsc104Callback()` method.
- Extended `oscDispatch()`: the no-semicolon early-return path now
  checks for OSC 104 before returning; a new `case 104` in the switch
  handles the parameterised form.

Tests: 7 new tests in parser.test.ts covering BEL and ST terminators,
single index, multiple indices, boundary values (0/255), and the
no-callback no-throw guarantee.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(db-analytics): memory leaks, O(n) scans, nil panics, silent errors across 18 services (#821)

* Initial plan

* fix: Database & Analytics Services - memory leaks, performance, race conditions

- DocDB (#698): tag cleanup on all 5 delete paths; fix RemoveTagsFromResource
  slice aliasing; remove dead-code fisFailoverFaults field
- Neptune (#699): DeleteDBCluster cascades to orphaned instances/tags;
  DeleteDBInstance cleans up tags; fix RemoveTagsFromResource slice aliasing
- LakeFormation (#720): DeregisterResource now removes associated permissions
- TimestreamWrite (#721): DeleteDatabase/DeleteTable clean up ARN tags
- CloudControl (#746): GetResourceRequestStatus deletes terminal requests
  (SUCCESS/FAILED/CANCEL_COMPLETE) after reading to bound map size
- RDS Data (#767): cap executedStatements at 1000 entries
- S3 Tables (#782): add tableIndex (bucketARN::ns::name→ARN) for O(1) lookups
- TimestreamQuery (#724): add arnIndex (ARN→name) for O(1) lookups
- EMR (#725): add arnIndex (ARN→ID); janitor cleans arnIndex on cluster sweep
- Redshift Data (#766): cap statements at 1000 entries with FIFO eviction
- QLDB Session (#763): cap sessions at 10000 with oldest-first eviction
- Redshift (#685): use defer in SetDNSRegistrar for safe lock release
- DMS (#713): riToJSON sets non-nil ReplicationSubnetGroupIdentifier
- OpenSearch (#684): add ARN→name index for O(1) domain ARN lookups
- Glue (#705): GetDatabase/GetCrawler/GetJob return deep copies via helpers

Co-authored-by: agbishop <1376376+agbishop@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: complete remaining sub-tasks for database/analytics issue

- AppSync (#679): cache parsed schema in Schema.parsedSchema (*ast.Schema,
  set once in StartSchemaCreation, never modified); executeGraphQL uses cache
  to avoid re-parsing on every request
- OpenSearch (#684): handleAddTags/handleRemoveTags now propagate errors
  (404 ResourceNotFoundException or 400 ValidationException) instead of
  silently discarding them with _ = ...
- Athena (#704): GetWorkGroup and GetDataCatalog return deep copies with
  maps.Clone on Tags (and Parameters for DataCatalog) to prevent callers
  from mutating internal state via returned pointers

Co-authored-by: agbishop <1376376+agbishop@users.noreply.github.com>

* Changes before error encountered

Co-authored-by: agbishop <1376376+agbishop@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address all reviewer feedback - Tags.Close, deep-copy on store, Neptune tag cleanup, RDS Data memory fix, new tests

Co-authored-by: agbishop <1376376+agbishop@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix double-draw on welcome screen init

Don't pre-draw the static screen before animation — only calculate
layout. The static _draw_welcome_screen is now only called by the
SIGWINCH handler on resize, avoiding a flash of completed content
before the animation starts.

commit.message:
fix(datasets): silently drop degenerate `bboxes` before Alb. val (#825)

COCO annotations sometimes place a bounding box exactly on the image
boundary, resulting in x_min == x_max (or y_min == y_max) after
coordinate clipping. Albumentations' check_bboxes rejects these with
"x_max is less than or equal to x_min", crashing DataLoader workers.

Filter out zero-width/zero-height boxes in _apply_geometric_transform
before passing to Albumentations. Labels, idxs, and masks are updated
in lockstep; because idxs carries original indices, downstream
_filter_per_instance_fields still slices per-instance fields correctly
from the un-filtered target.

Adds two regression tests covering the bbox-only and bbox+masks cases.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: pre-commit-ci[bot] <66853113+pre-commit-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
